sections.title: REFERENCES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate references for an investigation report on {process_type} involving {product_name}. Include QA- investigation SOPs, TM- test methods, PR- processing procedures, batch records, and any relevant deviations. {description}

sections.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
sections.llm_prompt: Write a 1-paragraph executive summary of an investigation into {process_type} involving {product_name}. {description}. State the issue, the investigation approach, the probable root cause, and the conclusion in plain language suitable for senior management.

sections.title: BACKGROUND
sections.llm_prompt: Write the background section of an investigation report for {product_name}. Describe normal operating conditions, the triggering event, chronology of discovery, and prior history of similar events.

sections.title: SCOPE OF INVESTIGATION
sections.llm_prompt: Define the scope of an investigation into {process_type} of {product_name}. State what batches, equipment, time period, and systems are included, and what is explicitly excluded.

sections.title: Timeline of Events
sections.llm_prompt: Generate a timeline of events for an investigation into {process_type} on {product_name}. Include the chronological sequence from normal operation through discovery, containment, and investigation.

sections.title: Investigation Activities
sections.llm_prompt: Describe investigation activities performed for {process_type} involving {product_name}. Cover document review, interviews, re-analysis, instrument checks, environmental data, and analytical retesting with relevant TM- test methods.

sections.title: Root Cause Analysis
sections.llm_prompt: Perform a root cause analysis for an issue in {process_type} of {product_name}. Apply the 5-Why or Fishbone (6M: Man, Method, Machine, Material, Measurement, Environment) approach. Identify the most probable root cause and any contributing factors.

sections.title: Findings
sections.llm_prompt: Summarize the findings of the investigation into {product_name}. State confirmed facts vs ruled-out hypotheses. Distinguish evidence from inference.

sections.title: Impact & Conclusion
sections.llm_prompt: Write the impact assessment and conclusion of an investigation report for {product_name}. Cover product quality impact, patient safety impact, regulatory reportability, affected batches, and the disposition decision.

sections.title: CAPA Summary
sections.llm_prompt: Generate CAPA items stemming from an investigation into {process_type} of {product_name}. Include both event-specific and systemic actions.
